extra.reasoning: focal_weakness appears only in r6 (priority 100), which sets acuity := self_care (the lowest level) when severe_headache AND leg_swelling AND rash AND back_pain AND dyspnea AND focal_weakness are all true. Take the base input severe_headache=T, leg_swelling=T, rash=T, back_pain=T, dyspnea=T, vision_loss=F, focal_weakness=F. With focal_weakness=F, r6 does not fire; the matching rules are r1/r2/r5 (urgent, pri 2) and r4/r7 (emergent, pri 3), so the priority-3 emergent rules win and acuity=emergent. Now set focal_weakness=T with all else unchanged: r6's condition becomes fully satisfied and at priority 100 it dominates all other rules, forcing acuity=self_care. Thus setting focal_weakness true lowers acuity from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest), violating the monotonicity property. The witness_assignment gives the 'before' input (focal_weakness=false) from which flipping focal_weakness to true triggers the drop.
